description:
You are a capacity-planning engineer who has just logged in to a Linux server that has been running for several hours.  
    Management has asked you to deliver an immediate point-in-time snapshot of how system resources are being consumed so that they can decide whether to provision more hardware.

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/capacity_planning  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Inside that directory create a plain-text report called  
       /home/user/capacity_planning/report.log

    3. The report **must** follow *exactly* the structure shown below.  
       • All section headers are UPPER-CASE followed by a colon.  
       • Fields inside each section are separated by a single space.  
       • There must be **one and only one** blank line between sections.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    Required file content layout (literal headers, variable values):

    PROC_SUMMARY:
    <rank> <pid> <user> <cpu_percent> <mem_percent> <command>
    <rank> <pid> <user> <cpu_percent> <mem_percent> <command>
    <rank> <pid> <user> <cpu_percent> <mem_percent> <command>
    <rank> <pid> <user> <cpu_percent> <mem_percent> <command>
    <rank> <pid> <user> <cpu_percent> <mem_percent> <command>

    MEMORY:
    Total_kB:<value> Free_kB:<value> Available_kB:<value> Buffers_kB:<value> Cached_kB:<value>

    LOAD_AVERAGE:
    1min:<value> 5min:<value> 15min:<value>

    TIME_GENERATED:
    <YYYY-MM-DD> <HH:MM:SS>
    --------------------------------------------------------------------

    Explanation of each required element:

    • PROC_SUMMARY  
      - Capture the *five* currently running processes using the most CPU, in descending order.  
      - <rank> is 1-5.  
      - <pid> is the numeric PID.  
      - <user> is the process owner as shown by standard tools.  
      - <cpu_percent> and <mem_percent> are the numeric utilisation values with exactly one digit after the decimal point (example: 23.4).  
      - <command> is the executable or command name (no arguments).  

    • MEMORY  
      - Read /proc/meminfo. Write the values (in kB) for the keys:  
        MemTotal, MemFree, MemAvailable, Buffers, and Cached, mapping them to the labels shown above.  
      - Do not include other keys.  
      - Values must be integers with no thousands separator.

    • LOAD_AVERAGE  
      - Read /proc/loadavg or use an equivalent tool. Report the 1-minute, 5-minute, and 15-minute averages with two digits after the decimal point (example: 0.07).

    • TIME_GENERATED  
      - Use the system’s current local time in the 24-hour format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

    4. Make sure the file permissions are 0644 (-rw-r--r--).

    5. After creating the report, display its contents to the terminal so the requester can immediately verify it.

    The automated checker will look for:

    • Existence of /home/user/capacity_planning/report.log  
    • Exact spelling and order of the four section headers.  
    • Exactly five non-header lines in PROC_SUMMARY.  
    • Correct presence and ordering of all key names in MEMORY and LOAD_AVERAGE lines.  
    • A properly formatted timestamp line.  
    • Only one blank line between sections and a single trailing newline at EOF.

    No additional text, commentary, or banner lines should appear in report.log.  
    You may install or use any command-line utilities available in the container, but you must not request escalated (root) privileges.
truth:
<!-- Verification details known only to the automated grader                         -->
    <!-- ================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Report location and permissions                                                   -->
    path:/home/user/capacity_planning/report.log
    required_mode:0644

    <!-- Regular expressions the grader will use to validate each section                 -->
    proc_header_regex:^PROC_SUMMARY:\n$
    proc_line_regex:^[1-5]\s+[0-9]+\s+\S+\s+[0-9]+\.[0-9]\s+[0-9]+\.[0-9]\s+\S+\n$
    proc_expected_count:5

    memory_header_regex:^MEMORY:\n$
    memory_line_regex:^Total_kB:[0-9]+\s+Free_kB:[0-9]+\s+Available_kB:[0-9]+\s+Buffers_kB:[0-9]+\s+Cached_kB:[0-9]+\n$

    load_header_regex:^LOAD_AVERAGE:\n$
    load_line_regex:^1min:[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}\s+5min:[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}\s+15min:[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}\n$

    time_header_regex:^TIME_GENERATED:\n$
    time_line_regex:^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\s+[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\n$

    blank_line_between_sections:true
    trailing_newline:true
